Front:
Your organization has 500 Windows Server VMs running legacy applications with SQL Server dependencies. You need to migrate to Azure with minimal downtime and ability to test before final cutover. The solution must support automated dependency mapping.

A) Azure Migrate with Azure Site Recovery and dependency visualization
B) Manual VM export with AzCopy upload
C) Azure Database Migration Service with backup/restore
Back: A) Azure Migrate with Azure Site Recovery and dependency visualization
Extra: Azure Migrate provides assessment with dependency visualization (agent-based or agentless), ASR enables replication with test failover before production cutover, and minimal downtime during final migration. DMS is for databases only. Manual export lacks automation and dependency discovery. Azure Migrate is the comprehensive migration platform.

Front:
A microservices application requires API gateway functionality with OAuth 2.0 token validation, request/response transformation, and backend service discovery with automatic endpoint updates.

A) Azure API Management with JWT validation policies and backend service fabric integration
B) Azure Application Gateway with custom health probes
C) Azure Front Door with rules engine
Back: A) Azure API Management with JWT validation policies and backend service fabric integration
Extra: APIM validates JWT tokens with policies, transforms requests/responses via policies, and integrates with Service Fabric for automatic endpoint discovery. App Gateway is L7 load balancing without API-specific features. Front Door lacks API transformation. APIM is the API gateway for security, transformation, and service integration.

Front:
Your company stores 10PB of sensor data in Azure Data Lake. Queries access only the last 30 days of data (200GB) with occasional historical analysis. Minimize storage costs while maintaining query performance for recent data.

A) Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 with lifecycle management to Archive tier after 30 days
B) Azure Blob Storage Cool tier with CDN
C) Azure Cosmos DB with TTL and hot partition strategy
Back: A) Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 with lifecycle management to Archive tier after 30 days
Extra: ADLS Gen2 lifecycle management automatically transitions data to Archive tier (lowest cost) after 30 days while keeping recent data in Hot tier for fast queries. Archive rehydration for occasional historical queries is acceptable. Cool tier still expensive for 9.97PB rarely accessed. Cosmos DB TTL deletes data (doesn't archive).

Front:
An application requires geo-distributed writes with conflict resolution based on the highest value field (e.g., inventory count). Data must be available in 5 regions with automatic conflict handling.

A) Azure Cosmos DB multi-region writes with Last-Write-Wins using custom _ts
B) Azure Cosmos DB multi-region writes with custom conflict resolution stored procedure
C) Azure SQL Database with active geo-replication and merge replication
Back: B) Azure Cosmos DB multi-region writes with custom conflict resolution stored procedure
Extra: Custom conflict resolution stored procedure implements business logic (select highest value). LWW uses timestamp (not value-based). SQL geo-replication doesn't support multi-writes. Cosmos DB conflict resolution policies (LWW, custom procedure, custom async) enable business-specific conflict handling for multi-master scenarios.

Front:
A data pipeline must transform and load 5TB of daily data from on-premises SQL Server to Azure Synapse Analytics with incremental loading and data quality validation before loading.

A) Azure Data Factory with mapping data flows, change data capture, and data flow assert transformation
B) Azure Synapse Pipelines with PolyBase bulk load
C) SSIS packages running on Azure VMs
Back: A) Azure Data Factory with mapping data flows, change data capture, and data flow assert transformation
Extra: ADF mapping data flows provide visual ETL with transformations, CDC tracks incremental changes, assert transformation validates data quality rules. PolyBase is fast load but lacks CDC and validation. SSIS on VMs lacks cloud-native benefits. ADF provides comprehensive cloud ETL with built-in quality gates.

Front:
Your organization requires centralized logging for 1000+ Azure resources with long-term retention (5 years), cost optimization, and ability to query historical data infrequently.

A) Log Analytics workspace with Archive tier and restore for queries
B) Export logs to Blob Storage with lifecycle management
C) Stream logs to Event Hubs and store in Data Lake
Back: A) Log Analytics workspace with Archive tier and restore for queries
Extra: Log Analytics Archive tier offers low-cost long-term retention (up to 12 years), searchable when needed via restore to Analytics tier for querying. Blob export loses query capability without additional tools. Event Hubs adds complexity. Archive tier balances cost and functionality for compliance retention.

Front:
A video streaming application serves content to global users with requirements for 4K video delivery, <100ms latency, and geo-filtering to comply with content licensing restrictions.

A) Azure Front Door Premium with Microsoft CDN, geo-filtering rules, and custom caching
B) Azure CDN Standard with custom rules
C) Azure Traffic Manager with Application Gateway
Back: A) Azure Front Door Premium with Microsoft CDN, geo-filtering rules, and custom caching
Extra: Front Door Premium provides global CDN with edge caching (reduces latency), WAF with geo-filtering (content restriction by region), and Microsoft CDN tier optimized for large files. Standard CDN has limited features. Traffic Manager + App Gateway lacks CDN edge caching. Front Door Premium is the global media delivery solution.

Front:
Your company uses Azure DevOps for CI/CD pipelines deploying to 100 Azure App Service instances across 10 subscriptions. Deployments must use managed identities for authentication without storing credentials.

A) Service connections with managed identity (workload identity federation)
B) Service principals with certificate-based authentication stored in Azure Key Vault
C) Deployment credentials configured per App Service
Back: A) Service connections with managed identity (workload identity federation)
Extra: Workload identity federation enables Azure DevOps to authenticate using managed identity without secrets/certificates. Key Vault still stores certificates (violates no credentials requirement). Deployment credentials are per-app secrets (doesn't scale). Workload identity federation eliminates credential storage entirely for CI/CD scenarios.

Front:
A machine learning model training job requires 100 GPU-enabled VMs for 6 hours twice per week. The workload is fault-tolerant and can tolerate interruptions. Minimize costs.

A) Azure Batch with Spot VMs and job re-queuing on eviction
B) Azure Kubernetes Service with dedicated GPU node pools
C) Azure Machine Learning with dedicated compute clusters
Back: A) Azure Batch with Spot VMs and job re-queuing on eviction
Extra: Spot VMs offer up to 90% discount with eviction risk (acceptable for fault-tolerant workloads), Batch manages job scheduling with automatic re-queue on eviction, and autoscales to 0 between runs. Dedicated GPU pools lack cost savings. ML compute works but Batch + Spot optimizes cost for intermittent GPU workloads.

Front:
Your application requires distributed tracing across microservices (HTTP, gRPC, message queues) with correlation IDs, performance analysis, and automatic dependency mapping.

A) Application Insights with distributed tracing and application map
B) Azure Monitor Logs with custom correlation queries
C) Azure Service Bus with message tracking
Back: A) Application Insights with distributed tracing and application map
Extra: Application Insights provides automatic distributed tracing across protocols (HTTP, gRPC), message queue correlation, performance insights, and visual application map of dependencies. Monitor Logs requires manual correlation. Service Bus tracking is limited to messaging. App Insights is the APM solution for distributed tracing.

Front:
A web application experiences variable traffic (100-10,000 requests/second) with strict latency requirements (<50ms p99). The solution must autoscale rapidly and support in-place deployment updates.

A) Azure App Service Premium V3 with autoscale rules and deployment slots
B) Azure Container Apps with HTTP scaling
C) Azure Kubernetes Service with HPA and rolling updates
Back: A) Azure App Service Premium V3 with autoscale rules and deployment slots
Extra: Premium V3 provides fast vertical scaling, autoscale rules for horizontal scaling, deployment slots for zero-downtime updates, and managed infrastructure. Container Apps scale well but lack deployment slot feature. AKS requires more management overhead. App Service balances performance, scaling, and deployment features for this scenario.

Front:
Your organization requires centralized network monitoring across 50 VNets with flow logs, packet capture on demand, and connectivity troubleshooting between resources.

A) Network Watcher with NSG flow logs, packet capture, and connection monitor
B) Azure Monitor with network insights
C) Azure Firewall logs with Log Analytics
Back: A) Network Watcher with NSG flow logs, packet capture, and connection monitor
Extra: Network Watcher provides NSG flow logs (traffic analytics), on-demand packet capture, connection monitor (connectivity tests), IP flow verify, and next hop diagnostics. Monitor network insights shows topology but not packet-level diagnostics. Firewall logs are centralized but don't cover all VNets. Network Watcher is the network diagnostics suite.

Front:
A payment processing application must encrypt data at rest with customer-managed keys, rotate keys annually, and maintain an audit log of all key access with alerts on unauthorized attempts.

A) Azure Key Vault Premium with customer-managed keys, automated rotation, and diagnostic logs to Log Analytics with alert rules
B) Azure Key Vault Standard with manual key rotation
C) Azure Storage encryption with Microsoft-managed keys
Back: A) Azure Key Vault Premium with customer-managed keys, automated rotation, and diagnostic logs to Log Analytics with alert rules
Extra: Premium SKU provides HSM-backed customer-managed keys, Key Vault supports automated rotation policies, diagnostic logs to Log Analytics enable audit trail, alert rules detect anomalies. Standard lacks HSM. Microsoft-managed keys don't provide customer control. Premium KV provides the security controls for compliance requirements.

Front:
A data science team requires shared file storage accessible from Linux VMs with POSIX permissions, SMB and NFS protocol support, and integration with Azure Active Directory for access control.

A) Azure Files Premium with NFS 4.1 and Active Directory domain join
B) Azure NetApp Files with dual-protocol (SMB/NFS) and AD integration
C) Azure Blob Storage with NFSv3 support
Back: B) Azure NetApp Files with dual-protocol (SMB/NFS) and AD integration
Extra: Azure NetApp Files supports both SMB and NFS simultaneously (dual-protocol), integrates with AD for identity-based access control, and provides high throughput for data workloads. Files Premium NFS doesn't support AD integration. Blob NFS lacks SMB and POSIX permissions. NetApp Files is designed for enterprise hybrid NAS workloads.

Front:
Your company requires disaster recovery for a Kubernetes cluster running stateful applications with RPO 1 hour, RTO 4 hours, and ability to failover to a secondary region.

A) Velero backup to Azure Storage with scheduled backups and cross-region restore
B) AKS cluster replication with Azure Site Recovery
C) Azure Backup for AKS container volumes
Back: A) Velero backup to Azure Storage with scheduled backups and cross-region restore
Extra: Velero is the CNCF standard for Kubernetes backup, supports scheduled backups (meets RPO), backs up persistent volumes and cluster state to Storage (GRS for cross-region), and restores to secondary region (meets RTO). ASR doesn't support AKS. Azure Backup for AKS is limited. Velero is the proven K8s DR solution.

Front:
An application requires session state shared across multiple App Service instances with sub-5ms latency, automatic failover, and support for ASP.NET session state provider.

A) Azure Cache for Redis Premium with geo-replication and ASP.NET session state provider
B) Azure SQL Database with in-memory OLTP
C) Azure Cosmos DB with Session consistency
Back: A) Azure Cache for Redis Premium with geo-replication and ASP.NET session state provider
Extra: Redis Premium provides sub-ms latency, native ASP.NET session provider, geo-replication for failover, and shared state across instances. SQL in-memory has higher latency. Cosmos DB lacks native ASP.NET integration. Redis is purpose-built for session state with native framework support and ultra-low latency.

Front:
Your organization deploys infrastructure using ARM templates across 20 subscriptions. You need version control, approval workflows, and automated deployment with rollback capabilities.

A) Azure DevOps repos with ARM templates, YAML pipelines with approval gates, and what-if deployment validation
B) GitHub with manual ARM template deployment
C) Azure Blueprints with artifact versioning
Back: A) Azure DevOps repos with ARM templates, YAML pipelines with approval gates, and what-if deployment validation
Extra: DevOps provides Git version control, YAML pipelines for automated deployment, approval gates for governance, and ARM what-if validates changes before deployment (enables rollback decision). Manual GitHub deployment lacks automation. Blueprints are for initial environment setup, not CI/CD. DevOps provides comprehensive IaC pipeline.

Front:
A web API must authenticate requests using JWT tokens issued by Azure AD with validation of specific claims (roles, groups) and rate limiting per user.

A) Azure API Management with validate-jwt policy and rate-limit-by-key policy
B) Azure AD Conditional Access with token lifetime policies
C) Azure App Service built-in authentication
Back: A) Azure API Management with validate-jwt policy and rate-limit-by-key policy
Extra: APIM validate-jwt policy checks token signature and claims, rate-limit-by-key enforces limits per user/token claim. Conditional Access controls sign-in, not API-level authorization. App Service auth provides basic authentication but not claim validation or rate limiting. APIM provides fine-grained API security policies.

Front:
A financial application requires audit logging of all data access and modifications with immutable logs for 7 years and alerts on anomalous access patterns.

A) Azure Monitor diagnostic settings to Log Analytics with immutable storage and anomaly detection queries
B) Application logging to Blob Storage with WORM policy
C) Azure SQL Database auditing with blob storage retention
Back: A) Azure Monitor diagnostic settings to Log Analytics with immutable storage and anomaly detection queries
Extra: Diagnostic settings capture data plane operations, Log Analytics enables querying and anomaly detection (ML-based), table-level immutability prevents tampering, and alert rules detect patterns. Blob WORM works but lacks querying. SQL auditing is database-specific. Monitor + Log Analytics provides comprehensive audit with analytics.

Front:
Your application requires communication between Azure VNets and AWS VPCs with encryption and minimal latency over the internet.

A) Azure VPN Gateway with site-to-site VPN to AWS Virtual Private Gateway
B) ExpressRoute with AWS Direct Connect via colocation
C) Azure Front Door with AWS CloudFront integration
Back: A) Azure VPN Gateway with site-to-site VPN to AWS Virtual Private Gateway
Extra: S2S VPN connects Azure VNet to AWS VPC over IPsec (encrypted internet tunnel), supports BGP routing, and works without physical circuit. ExpressRoute + Direct Connect requires colocation (complex/expensive). Front Door is for web traffic not VNet connectivity. VPN Gateway provides cross-cloud private connectivity over internet.

Front:
A database workload has unpredictable traffic patterns with idle periods (hours) and occasional spikes (10,000 concurrent queries). The database is 500GB. Minimize costs while maintaining performance during spikes.

A) Azure SQL Database Serverless with auto-pause and auto-scale
B) Azure SQL Database provisioned with manual scaling
C) Azure SQL Managed Instance with Business Critical tier
Back: A) Azure SQL Database Serverless with auto-pause and auto-scale
Extra: Serverless automatically scales compute based on load, auto-pauses during idle periods (no compute charges), and resumes on demand. Provisioned tiers charge continuously. Managed Instance is more expensive without specific requirements. Serverless optimizes cost for variable workloads with idle periods.

Front:
An IoT solution processes telemetry from 1 million devices with requirements for device identity, secure provisioning at scale, and ability to disable/revoke devices remotely.

A) Azure IoT Hub with Device Provisioning Service (DPS) and device twin properties
B) Azure Event Hubs with shared access signatures
C) Azure Service Bus with connection string per device
Back: A) Azure IoT Hub with Device Provisioning Service (DPS) and device twin properties
Extra: IoT Hub provides device identity registry, DPS enables zero-touch provisioning, device twins support remote configuration/disable. Event Hubs lacks device identity. Service Bus connection strings don't scale to millions of devices. IoT Hub is purpose-built for device management, security, and telemetry at scale.

Front:
Your company requires that all Azure Storage accounts must use private endpoints and disable public network access. The policy must apply automatically to new and existing accounts.

A) Azure Policy with DeployIfNotExists effect for private endpoints and Deny effect for public access
B) ARM templates with required parameters
C) Azure Automation runbook to audit and configure storage accounts
Back: A) Azure Policy with DeployIfNotExists effect for private endpoints and Deny effect for public access
Extra: DeployIfNotExists automatically creates private endpoints if missing (remediates existing accounts), Deny prevents public access enablement on new/existing accounts. ARM templates only affect new deployments via that template. Automation runbook is reactive. Policy provides proactive and continuous enforcement of security baselines.

Front:
A web application requires end-to-end encryption including data in transit between load balancer and backend VMs with certificate management and automatic renewal.

A) Azure Application Gateway with end-to-end TLS and Key Vault integration for certificate management
B) Azure Load Balancer with SSL offload
C) Azure Front Door with backend encryption
Back: A) Azure Application Gateway with end-to-end TLS and Key Vault integration for certificate management
Extra: Application Gateway supports end-to-end TLS (re-encryption to backends), Key Vault integration auto-renews certificates, and provides L7 features. Standard Load Balancer doesn't support SSL. Front Door encrypts to edge but requires additional config for backend encryption. App Gateway provides complete E2E TLS with managed certificates.

Front:
Your organization needs to implement least privilege access for developers to create VMs but prohibit creation of public IPs and restrict VM sizes to cost-effective options.

A) Custom RBAC role with VM create permissions and Azure Policy to deny public IP and expensive VM SKUs
B) Contributor role with Conditional Access restrictions
C) Virtual Machine Contributor role only
Back: A) Custom RBAC role with VM create permissions and Azure Policy to deny public IP and expensive VM SKUs
Extra: Custom role grants specific VM creation actions, Policy Deny effect prevents public IP creation and large VM SKUs, combination provides least privilege with guardrails. Contributor is too broad. VM Contributor includes all VM operations without restrictions. Custom RBAC + Policy implements least privilege with cost/security controls.

Front:
A video processing application requires GPU compute that runs for 2-8 hours triggered by blob uploads. Jobs can tolerate interruptions and restart from checkpoints. Minimize costs.

A) Azure Batch with Spot VMs, blob trigger via Event Grid, and task restart policies
B) Azure Functions with GPU-enabled Premium plan
C) Dedicated GPU VMs with scheduled start/stop
Back: A) Azure Batch with Spot VMs, blob trigger via Event Grid, and task restart policies
Extra: Batch manages job scheduling with task-level checkpoint/restart, Spot VMs provide 90% discount, Event Grid triggers jobs on blob upload, autoscaling to 0 between jobs. Functions doesn't support GPU workloads at scale. Dedicated VMs lack job orchestration and cost optimization. Batch + Spot + Event Grid is optimal for GPU batch workloads.

Front:
Your company requires monitoring of Azure AD sign-in patterns with alerts on risky sign-ins, detection of impossible travel, and automated response (block user, require MFA step-up).

A) Azure AD Identity Protection with risk policies and automated remediation actions
B) Azure AD logs with custom Log Analytics queries and alert rules
C) Azure Sentinel with sign-in analytics
Back: A) Azure AD Identity Protection with risk policies and automated remediation actions
Extra: Identity Protection provides ML-based risk detection (impossible travel, atypical travel, anonymous IP), risk-based Conditional Access for automated response (block, require MFA), and investigation tools. Custom queries lack ML detection. Sentinel works but Identity Protection is purpose-built for Azure AD risk detection with native integration.

Front:
A data warehouse requires separation of compute and storage, ability to pause compute during non-business hours, and support for materialized views with automatic query optimization.

A) Azure Synapse Analytics dedicated SQL pool with pause/resume and result set caching
B) Azure SQL Database with read scale-out
C) Azure Databricks with Delta Lake
Back: A) Azure Synapse Analytics dedicated SQL pool with pause/resume and result set caching
Extra: Synapse dedicated pool separates storage (charged always) from compute (pause during idle), supports materialized views, result set cache, and adaptive query optimization. SQL Database doesn't pause (always billed). Databricks is for Spark not SQL warehousing. Synapse provides pause/resume for cost control in data warehousing.

Front:
An application must process messages from a queue with guaranteed delivery, ability to defer messages for later processing, and automatic dead-lettering after 5 failed delivery attempts.

A) Azure Service Bus queue with PeekLock, message deferral, and max delivery count
B) Azure Storage Queue with visibility timeout
C) Azure Event Grid with exponential retry
Back: A) Azure Service Bus queue with PeekLock, message deferral, and max delivery count
Extra: Service Bus supports PeekLock (guaranteed delivery), defer() for manual processing scheduling, max delivery count triggers automatic dead-letter, and rich retry policies. Storage Queue has simpler semantics without deferral. Event Grid is for pub/sub events not queue processing. Service Bus provides enterprise messaging features.

Front:
Your organization requires cross-region replication of container images with automatic geo-replication and webhook notifications on image push for CI/CD pipelines.

A) Azure Container Registry Premium with geo-replication and webhooks
B) Azure Container Registry Basic with manual replication
C) Docker Hub with Azure integration
Back: A) Azure Container Registry Premium with geo-replication and webhooks
Extra: Premium SKU provides geo-replication (replicate to multiple regions automatically), webhooks trigger on push/delete for automation, content trust for image signing, and 100Gbps+ throughput. Basic lacks geo-replication. Docker Hub is external without Azure integration. ACR Premium provides enterprise container registry with global distribution.

Front:
A SaaS application must isolate customer data in separate databases while maintaining centralized management, connection pooling, and cost efficiency for 1000 customers.

A) Azure SQL Database with elastic pools and database-per-tenant model
B) Azure SQL Database with row-level security for shared database
C) Azure SQL Managed Instance with separate instances per customer
Back: A) Azure SQL Database with elastic pools and database-per-tenant model
Extra: Elastic pool shares resources across databases (cost efficient), database-per-tenant provides strong isolation, centralized management via automation, and automatic scaling. RLS provides soft isolation (not physical separation). Managed Instance per customer is prohibitively expensive. Elastic pools balance isolation with cost for multi-tenant SaaS.

Front:
Your company requires real-time monitoring of Azure costs with alerts when spending exceeds budget thresholds and automatic tagging of untagged resources for cost allocation.

A) Azure Cost Management with budgets, alert rules, and Azure Policy Append effect for tags
B) Azure Advisor cost recommendations
C) Azure Monitor metrics with custom cost queries
Back: A) Azure Cost Management with budgets, alert rules, and Azure Policy Append effect for tags
Extra: Cost Management provides budget creation with alert thresholds, cost analysis by tags, and integration with Policy for tag governance. Advisor provides recommendations (not real-time alerts). Monitor doesn't track costs directly. Cost Management + Policy is the native Azure cost governance solution with alerting.

Front:
A web application must handle 50,000 concurrent WebSocket connections with message broadcasting to specific user groups and automatic reconnection on transient failures.

A) Azure SignalR Service with group management and automatic reconnection
B) Azure Web PubSub service with client SDK
C) Self-hosted SignalR on App Service
Back: A) Azure SignalR Service with group management and automatic reconnection
Extra: SignalR Service is managed, scales to 100K+ connections, provides group/user targeting for broadcasts, and client SDK handles reconnection. Web PubSub works but SignalR has better client library integration. Self-hosted doesn't scale without complex infrastructure. SignalR Service provides managed real-time messaging at scale.

Front:
Your organization deploys multi-tier applications repeatedly across environments (dev, test, prod). You need blueprints that include RBAC assignments, policy assignments, ARM templates, and resource groups.

A) Azure Blueprints with artifacts (RBAC, policies, templates, resource groups)
B) ARM templates with nested templates
C) Azure DevOps pipelines with manual deployments
Back: A) Azure Blueprints with artifacts (RBAC, policies, templates, resource groups)
Extra: Blueprints package governance artifacts (policies, RBAC) with infrastructure (ARM templates, resource groups), versioned and reusable across subscriptions. ARM templates can't assign policies/RBAC natively. Pipelines require manual orchestration. Blueprints provide declarative governance + infrastructure for repeatable environment setup.

Front:
A data lake stores raw sensor data (JSON) that must be queried using SQL syntax without data movement. Queries are ad-hoc and infrequent (10/month). Minimize costs.

A) Azure Synapse serverless SQL pool with OPENROWSET querying Data Lake JSON files
B) Load data into Synapse dedicated pool
C) Azure Databricks with external tables
Back: A) Azure Synapse serverless SQL pool with OPENROWSET querying Data Lake JSON files
Extra: Serverless SQL charges per TB scanned (no compute when idle), OPENROWSET queries JSON files in place, supports SQL syntax. Dedicated pool charges for provisioned resources. Databricks has cluster costs even with auto-termination. Serverless SQL is optimal for infrequent ad-hoc queries over data lakes without data movement.

Front:
An application requires key-value storage with TTL, atomic increment operations, pub/sub messaging, and sub-millisecond latency for 100,000 ops/second.

A) Azure Cache for Redis Premium with clustering and pub/sub
B) Azure Cosmos DB with partition keys
C) Azure Table Storage with SAS tokens
Back: A) Azure Cache for Redis Premium with clustering and pub/sub
Extra: Redis provides native key-value with TTL, atomic operations (INCR), pub/sub channels, sub-ms latency, and clustering for scale. Cosmos DB has single-digit ms (not sub-ms) and costs more. Table Storage has much higher latency and lacks atomic ops/pub-sub. Redis is purpose-built for this high-performance caching/messaging scenario.

Front:
Your company requires centralized secrets management for 100 applications with automatic rotation every 90 days, audit logging of access, and RBAC for secret management.

A) Azure Key Vault with rotation policies, diagnostic logs, and access policies
B) Azure App Configuration for all secrets
C) Environment variables in Azure DevOps
Back: A) Azure Key Vault with rotation policies, diagnostic logs, and access policies
Extra: Key Vault provides automated rotation policies (certificates, secrets), audit logs to Monitor, RBAC/access policies for granular permissions, and versioning. App Configuration is for app settings not secrets. Environment variables in pipelines lack rotation and audit. Key Vault is the secrets management solution with governance.

Front:
A machine learning model serves predictions via REST API with requirements for A/B testing (traffic split), automatic scaling based on request rate, and GPU inference.

A) Azure Machine Learning managed endpoints with traffic routing and GPU compute
B) Azure Functions with GPU support
C) Azure Kubernetes Service with Istio service mesh
Back: A) Azure Machine Learning managed endpoints with traffic routing and GPU compute
Extra: ML managed endpoints provide native A/B testing via traffic splits, autoscaling based on requests/CPU, GPU compute support, and monitoring. Functions doesn't support GPU at scale. AKS + Istio works but requires more management. ML managed endpoints are purpose-built for model serving with traffic management.

Front:
Your organization requires disaster recovery for on-premises file servers (10TB) to Azure with RPO 1 hour, RTO 4 hours, and ability to perform DR drills without impacting production.

A) Azure File Sync with test failover to Azure Files shares
B) Azure Backup for files with restore to Azure VMs
C) Azure Site Recovery for physical servers hosting file shares
Back: A) Azure File Sync with test failover to Azure Files shares
Extra: File Sync replicates on-premises files to Azure Files continuously (sub-hour RPO), test failover mounts Azure Files without affecting production, and RTO meets 4 hours. Backup requires restore time (may exceed RTO). ASR is for VMs not file shares. File Sync provides continuous replication and DR for file workloads.

Front:
A gaming application requires leaderboard updates with millions of concurrent users, sub-5ms latency, automatic ranking, and persistence for offline analysis.

A) Azure Cache for Redis with sorted sets persisted to Azure Storage via export
B) Azure Cosmos DB with composite indexes
C) Azure SQL Database with in-memory OLTP
Back: A) Azure Cache for Redis with sorted sets persisted to Azure Storage via export
Extra: Redis sorted sets provide sub-ms leaderboard operations with automatic ranking, scales horizontally, RDB/AOF export to Storage for analysis. Cosmos DB has higher latency. SQL in-memory can't match Redis for this pattern. Redis is optimal for real-time leaderboards with persistence option for analytics.

Front:
Your company requires network security controls with layer-7 inspection, IDS/IPS capabilities, and centralized management for traffic between Azure and on-premises.

A) Azure Firewall Premium with IDPS and TLS inspection
B) Azure Firewall Standard with threat intelligence
C) Network Security Groups with application security groups
Back: A) Azure Firewall Premium with IDPS and TLS inspection
Extra: Premium SKU provides IDPS (intrusion detection/prevention), TLS inspection (L7 deep packet inspection), URL filtering, and threat intelligence. Standard lacks IDPS/TLS inspection. NSGs operate at L4 only. Premium Firewall provides next-gen firewall capabilities for advanced threat protection.

Front:
A data warehouse must load 10TB of data nightly from on-premises SQL Server with minimal network impact and transformations before loading into Synapse Analytics.

A) Azure Data Factory with self-hosted IR, staging to Data Lake, and mapping data flows to Synapse
B) PolyBase direct load from on-premises
C) ExpressRoute with bulk copy
Back: A) Azure Data Factory with self-hosted IR, staging to Data Lake, and mapping data flows to Synapse
Extra: Self-hosted IR accesses on-premises SQL, stages data in Data Lake (network optimization via compression), mapping data flows transform data, and parallel copy to Synapse. PolyBase can't directly access on-premises without network path. ExpressRoute helps but lacks ETL logic. ADF provides complete hybrid ETL pipeline.

Front:
An application must respond to Azure Storage blob events (upload, delete) within seconds with guaranteed delivery and ability to batch process multiple events together.

A) Event Grid with Azure Functions consumption plan and batch trigger
B) Blob Storage change feed with polling
C) Azure Logic Apps with blob trigger
Back: A) Event Grid with Azure Functions consumption plan and batch trigger
Extra: Event Grid delivers blob events in real-time (<30s typically), Functions batch trigger processes multiple events, and guaranteed delivery with retry. Change feed is polling-based (not real-time). Logic Apps work but Functions provide better performance/cost for event processing. Event Grid + Functions is the event-driven blob processing pattern.

Front:
Your organization requires centralized identity for on-premises Active Directory users to access Azure resources with SSO and without password hash synchronization to the cloud.

A) Azure AD Connect with Pass-through Authentication (PTA) and SSO
B) Azure AD Connect with Password Hash Sync
C) Active Directory Federation Services (ADFS)
Back: A) Azure AD Connect with Pass-through Authentication (PTA) and SSO
Extra: PTA authenticates against on-premises AD without syncing password hashes to cloud, provides SSO, and installs lightweight agents. Password Hash Sync violates the no-hash requirement. ADFS works but adds complexity compared to PTA. PTA balances security (no cloud password hashes) with simplicity (no ADFS infrastructure).

Front:
A microservices application requires service mesh capabilities including mTLS between services, traffic splitting for canary deployments, and distributed tracing without code changes.

A) Azure Kubernetes Service with Istio service mesh add-on
B) Azure API Management for microservices
C) Azure Front Door with backend routing
Back: A) Azure Kubernetes Service with Istio service mesh add-on
Extra: Istio provides service mesh with automatic mTLS encryption, traffic management (canary/blue-green), distributed tracing, and sidecar proxies (no code changes). APIM is API gateway not service mesh. Front Door is external load balancing. Istio provides comprehensive service mesh for microservices observability and security.

Front:
Your company requires backup of Azure SQL Managed Instance with long-term retention of monthly backups for 10 years and ability to restore to specific points in time within the last 7 days.

A) Automated backups with PITR enabled and LTR policy configured
B) Manual BACPAC exports to Blob Storage
C) Geo-replication for backup purposes
Back: A) Automated backups with PITR enabled and LTR policy configured
Extra: Managed Instance automated backups provide PITR (7-35 days configurable), LTR supports weekly/monthly/yearly backups up to 10 years. BACPAC is manual and slow for large databases. Geo-replication is for availability not backups. Automated backups with LTR provide complete backup/retention solution built into the service.

Front:
A big data processing pipeline requires orchestration of data flows with dependency management, conditional execution, and integration with on-premises data sources.

A) Azure Data Factory with control flow activities, parameters, and self-hosted integration runtime
B) Azure Logic Apps with connectors
C) Azure Functions with timer triggers
Back: A) Azure Data Factory with control flow activities, parameters, and self-hosted integration runtime
Extra: ADF provides visual pipeline orchestration with control flow (if, foreach, until), activity dependencies, parameters for reusability, and self-hosted IR for on-premises access. Logic Apps are for workflow automation not data pipelines. Functions require code for orchestration. ADF is purpose-built for ETL/ELT orchestration.

Front:
Your application requires full-text search with faceted navigation, autocomplete suggestions, and search ranking based on business rules (boost specific products).

A) Azure Cognitive Search with scoring profiles and suggester configuration
B) Azure SQL Database full-text search
C) Cosmos DB with composite indexes
Back: A) Azure Cognitive Search with scoring profiles and suggester configuration
Extra: Cognitive Search provides full-text search with analyzers, faceted navigation, suggesters for autocomplete, and scoring profiles for custom ranking. SQL full-text is less rich. Cosmos indexes don't provide full-text. Cognitive Search is purpose-built search engine with enterprise features like relevance tuning.

Front:
A web application must enforce that users can only access resources from company-managed devices with valid certificates, require MFA for sensitive operations, and block access from risky locations.

A) Azure AD Conditional Access with device compliance, MFA policy, and location-based conditions
B) Azure Front Door with WAF rules
C) Network Security Groups with IP allowlists
Back: A) Azure AD Conditional Access with device compliance, MFA policy, and location-based conditions
Extra: Conditional Access enforces device compliance (managed device + certificate check), MFA requirements based on application/risk, and location-based allow/block. Front Door WAF operates at network level not identity. NSGs lack identity/device awareness. Conditional Access provides identity-driven access control with contextual policies.

Front:
Your company requires centralized certificate management for 200 web applications with automatic renewal, deployment to App Services and VMs, and rotation without downtime.

A) Azure Key Vault with certificate auto-renewal and App Service/VM extensions for deployment
B) Let's Encrypt with manual renewal scripts
C) Self-signed certificates with Storage account storage
Back: A) Azure Key Vault with certificate auto-renewal and App Service/VM extensions for deployment
Extra: Key Vault provides CA integration for auto-renewal (e.g., DigiCert), App Service imports from Key Vault automatically, VM extensions fetch certificates, and versioning enables zero-downtime rotation. Manual Let's Encrypt doesn't scale. Self-signed lacks CA trust. Key Vault is centralized certificate lifecycle management.

Front:
A stateless web API requires deployment with blue-green strategy, automatic rollback on error rate exceeding 5%, and traffic ramping (10%-50%-100% over 3 days).

A) Azure App Service deployment slots with traffic routing, Application Insights metrics, and automated slot swaps
B) Azure Kubernetes Service with Flagger progressive delivery
C) Azure Front Door with weighted backend pools
Back: A) Azure App Service deployment slots with traffic routing, Application Insights metrics, and automated slot swaps
Extra: Deployment slots enable blue-green, traffic routing supports gradual ramp (custom %), App Insights metrics detect errors, auto-swap can integrate with monitoring for automated rollback. AKS Flagger works but adds complexity. Front Door requires manual backend management. App Service slots are native progressive delivery.

Front:
Your organization requires VM backups encrypted with customer-managed keys, immutable backups preventing deletion for 5 years, and ability to restore files from specific backup points.

A) Azure Backup with customer-managed keys, vault immutability, and file-level restore
B) VM disk snapshots with managed disk encryption
C) Azure Site Recovery for backup purposes
Back: A) Azure Backup with customer-managed keys, vault immutability, and file-level restore
Extra: Azure Backup supports CMK encryption, vault immutability locks backups (WORM compliance), and file-level restore from VM backups. Snapshots lack centralized management and immutability. ASR is for replication not backups. Azure Backup provides enterprise backup with encryption, compliance, and granular restore options.

Front:
A data processing pipeline requires exactly-once message processing semantics with ordering guaranteed per partition key and 90-day message retention for reprocessing.

A) Azure Event Hubs with partitioning, offset management, and extended retention
B) Azure Service Bus with sessions
C) Azure Storage Queue with deduplication
Back: A) Azure Event Hubs with partitioning, offset management, and extended retention
Extra: Event Hubs provides per-partition ordering, consumer offset management enables exactly-once (idempotent processing), and supports 90-day retention. Service Bus sessions are for ordering but shorter retention (14 days max). Storage Queue lacks ordering guarantees. Event Hubs is the streaming platform for ordered, retained event processing.

Front:
Your company requires hosting legacy Windows services (Windows Services, scheduled tasks) in Azure with domain join capabilities and ability to RDP for management.

A) Azure Virtual Machines with Active Directory domain join and just-in-time VM access
B) Azure Container Instances with Windows containers
C) Azure App Service on Windows
Back: A) Azure Virtual Machines with Active Directory domain join and just-in-time VM access
Extra: VMs support Windows Services, domain join to AD (on-premises or Azure AD DS), RDP access, and JIT VM access for secure RDP. Container Instances don't support domain join or Windows Services. App Service doesn't support Windows Services. VMs provide full Windows Server capabilities for legacy workloads.

Front:
A web application requires content delivery with edge caching, custom domain SSL, and cache purging based on application events (e.g., product price changes).

A) Azure Front Door with custom domains, managed certificates, and purge API integration
B) Azure CDN Standard with custom SSL
C) Azure Blob Storage with static website hosting
Back: A) Azure Front Door with custom domains, managed certificates, and purge API integration
Extra: Front Door provides global CDN with custom domain support, free managed certs, and purge API for programmatic cache invalidation. CDN Standard works but Front Door has better global coverage and features. Blob static hosting lacks CDN edge caching. Front Door combines CDN, SSL management, and programmable cache control.

Front:
Your organization requires network connectivity between 50 Azure VNets across 10 regions with centralized routing, security filtering, and simplified management.

A) Azure Virtual WAN with secured hubs (Azure Firewall) and branch connectivity
B) Hub-spoke topology with VNet peering and UDRs
C) Full mesh VNet peering
Back: A) Azure Virtual WAN with secured hubs (Azure Firewall) and branch connectivity
Extra: Virtual WAN provides any-to-any global transit, secured hubs integrate Firewall for centralized security, automatic routing, and SD-WAN integration. Hub-spoke requires manual peering and routing for each connection. Full mesh doesn't scale (50 VNets = 1225 peerings). Virtual WAN simplifies global network topology.

Front:
A reporting application queries data from multiple SQL databases (Azure SQL, on-premises SQL, Oracle) and requires a unified query layer with minimal latency and no data movement.

A) Azure Synapse Analytics with external tables via PolyBase and Managed VNet for on-premises access
B) Azure Data Factory with copy activities
C) SQL Server Linked Servers
Back: A) Azure Synapse Analytics with external tables via PolyBase and Managed VNet for on-premises access
Extra: Synapse PolyBase queries external data sources (SQL, Oracle) without data movement, T-SQL interface, Managed VNet enables secure on-premises access. Data Factory is ETL (requires data movement). Linked Servers don't scale and have security risks. Synapse provides unified query layer for heterogeneous data sources.

Front:
Your company requires monitoring of custom application metrics (e.g., shopping cart abandonment rate) with alerting when thresholds are exceeded and visualization in dashboards.

A) Application Insights with custom metrics API, metric alerts, and Azure dashboards
B) Azure Monitor Logs with custom tables
C) Azure Cosmos DB change feed with analytics
Back: A) Application Insights with custom metrics API, metric alerts, and Azure dashboards
Extra: Application Insights custom metrics API tracks application-specific metrics, metric alerts trigger on thresholds, and dashboards visualize trends. Monitor Logs provides logging not metric time-series. Cosmos change feed is for data changes not metrics. App Insights extends beyond infrastructure to application business metrics.

Front:
A database requires read scale-out to handle reporting workloads without impacting primary write workload, with near real-time data (few seconds lag acceptable).

A) Azure SQL Database Business Critical with read scale-out replicas
B) Azure SQL Database with geo-replication for reporting
C) Azure Synapse Analytics with dedicated pool
Back: A) Azure SQL Database Business Critical with read scale-out replicas
Extra: Business Critical tier includes secondary replicas for high availability, read scale-out routes read-only queries to replicas (offloads primary), with seconds lag. Geo-replication is for DR with longer lag. Synapse is for data warehousing not OLTP scale-out. Read scale-out separates transactional and analytical workloads.

Front:
An application requires session affinity (sticky sessions) at the load balancer level with support for HTTP/2, WebSocket protocols, and custom health probes.

A) Azure Application Gateway with cookie-based affinity and custom health probes
B) Azure Standard Load Balancer with source IP affinity
C) Azure Traffic Manager with session affinity
Back: A) Azure Application Gateway with cookie-based affinity and custom health probes
Extra: Application Gateway provides L7 features: cookie-based session affinity, HTTP/2 and WebSocket support, and custom health probes (path/interval/threshold). Standard LB is L4 (TCP/UDP only). Traffic Manager is DNS-based without session affinity. App Gateway delivers application-level load balancing with session persistence.

Front:
Your company requires encrypting all data in Azure Storage accounts with keys managed and rotated by the company's HSM, with audit logs of all key usage.

A) Azure Storage encryption with customer-managed keys in Key Vault (CMK from HSM) and diagnostic logging
B) Azure Storage Service Encryption with Microsoft-managed keys
C) Client-side encryption before upload
Back: A) Azure Storage encryption with customer-managed keys in Key Vault (CMK from HSM) and diagnostic logging
Extra: CMK allows importing HSM keys to Key Vault (Premium SKU), Storage encrypts with CMK, Key Vault diagnostic logs track usage. Microsoft-managed keys don't provide customer control. Client-side encryption requires application changes and key management. CMK provides encryption control with centralized HSM key management.

Front:
A mobile app backend requires offline synchronization with conflict resolution, user authentication, and automatic data sync when connectivity resumes.

A) Azure Mobile Apps with offline sync SDK and Azure AD authentication
B) Azure Cosmos DB with client SDK
C) Azure SQL Database with custom sync logic
Back: A) Azure Mobile Apps with offline sync SDK and Azure AD authentication
Extra: Mobile Apps provides offline sync with conflict resolution, integrates with Azure AD for authentication, and automatic sync on reconnection. Cosmos client SDK requires manual offline logic. SQL requires custom sync implementation. Mobile Apps is purpose-built for mobile offline scenarios with built-in sync framework.

Front:
Your organization requires protecting Azure SQL databases from SQL injection, data exfiltration, and anomalous access patterns with automatic threat detection and alerting.

A) Azure Defender for SQL with vulnerability assessment and threat detection
B) Azure Firewall with SQL database rules
C) SQL Database auditing with Log Analytics
Back: A) Azure Defender for SQL with vulnerability assessment and threat detection
Extra: Defender for SQL provides advanced threat protection (SQL injection, unusual access), vulnerability assessment scans, security alerts, and recommendations. Firewall operates at network level not SQL query level. Auditing logs activity but doesn't detect threats. Defender is the comprehensive SQL security solution with ML-based detection.
